data.title: American Airlines Stock (AAL) Falls 10% as Jet Fuel Prices Cause Turbulence
data.description:
The stock of American Airlines (AAL) is down 10% in afternoon trading on July 23 after the carrier said that rising jet fuel costs will likely cause it to post a financial loss this year.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Management at American Airlines said following the company's latest financial results that the carrier could post an adjusted loss per share of as much as $0.65 in 2026 due to soaring jet fuel costs. Despite a strong second-quarter print, American Airlines lowered its full-year 2026 earnings outlook, citing higher fuel costs as the Iran war pushes crude oil prices back above $100 a barrel.

Jet fuel is typically an airline's second biggest expense after labor costs. American and other carriers have raised airfares this year to try and offset the increased fuel expenses. However, executives at American Airlines said the fare increases aren't enough to fully offset this year's spike in jet fuel.

A Strong Print from American Airlines

Dallas, Texas-based American Airlines, which is the world's largest carrier, reported second-quarter earnings per share (EPS) of $0.15, which handily beat the $0.03 forecast on Wall Street. Revenue in the spring quarter totaled $16.74 billion, which topped the consensus estimate among analysts of $16.71 billion U.S. Sales were up 16% from a year ago.

American Airlines said that any relief it got from declining fuels costs was short-lived as the U.S. and Iran have resumed their war in recent weeks. The airline said that it plans to expand its flying capacity by as much as 5% in the current third quarter, add more premium first-class seats, and order new wide-body aircraft to help improve its outlook heading into 2027.

Comparing Airline Stocks

Below is a chart comparing the stocks of the three largest U.S. airlines: American, Delta (DAL) and United (UAL). As one can see below, DAL and UAL stocks carry Strong Buy recommendations while AAL stock has a Moderate Buy rating. United's stock has the most upside potential of the three securities.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Arthur Hayes' BitMEX Shuts Down After 11 Years, BMEX Token Crashes 90%
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange BitMEX has announced it will officially shut down permanently on Wednesday, September 23, 2026, after more than 11 years of operations. The exchange said it has stopped accepting new users after a business review led to the shutdown decision. Meanwhile, BMEX, the native token of BitMEX, crashed 90% just a few hours after the announcement.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

BitMEX Announces Shutdown as BMEX Token Tumbles

BitMEX said its parent company, HDR Global Trading Limited, decided to shut down the exchange after a strategic review of the business and the crypto market. Despite the closure, the company said customer funds remain safe and urged users to withdraw their assets before September 23.

Notably, BitMEX launched in 2014 and became one of the largest crypto exchanges after launching 100x leveraged perpetual futures, a trading product that lets users amplify their positions by up to 100 times. The company also said it never lost customer funds to hacks throughout its operations.

According to crypto data-tracking company Kaiko, BitMEX controls less than 0.01% of the crypto exchange market, a tiny fraction of worldwide trading activity. As a result, analysts say its closure is unlikely to have a major impact on the wider crypto trading market.

Arthur Hayes and CZ Reflect on BitMEX's Legacy

BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes thanked the company's employees, partners and customers, saying he was proud of what they built. He added that the exchange was shutting down responsibly on its own terms.

Changpeng Zhao (CZ), the former CEO of Binance, the world's largest exchange, described BitMEX as a pioneer that changed crypto derivatives trading by rolling out perpetual futures in 2014. He said the platform's Bitcoin (BTC-USD)-only deposits, once-daily withdrawals and multi-signature wallet system helped it avoid hacks throughout its operations.

CZ also said BitMEX struggled to recover after its founders pleaded guilty to the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) violations and were later pardoned by the U.S. President Donald Trump. He blamed the previous U.S. administration's "war on crypto" for the company's decline. The shutdown shows how difficult it has become for smaller crypto exchanges to compete and survive in the market.

Which Cryptocurrency Stock Is Best to Invest in Now?

BitMEX remains a private company and does not have publicly traded stock. However, investors interested in the crypto sector can consider publicly traded companies such as Block Inc (XYZ), Robinhood (HOOD), and Strategy (MSTR). (See their stock forecasts)
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Sinks as Oil Surges above $100 on New Energy Chokepoint Threat
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Thursday as escalating tensions in the Middle East continue to drive energy prices higher.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, surged above $100 per barrel for the first time since late May, reigniting inflation concerns. That came after the Iran-backed Houthi military group attacked two Saudi tankers in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The waterway handles roughly 12% of global seaborne oil flows. That puts another energy chokepoint, alongside the Strait of Hormuz, at risk.

In a Truth Social post, President Trump warned that both Iran and the Houthis would face "major military punishment" if Saudi tankers were to be targeted again. The warning comes as U.S.-Iran hostilities continue to escalate, with strikes on Iran reaching their 12th straight day.

Soaring oil prices have driven rate hike odds higher. By year-end, the odds of at least one hike rose to 92.5% from 90.2% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. In addition, the most likely outcome is for two hikes at 38.9%, followed by one hike at 31.3%.

Elsewhere, initial jobless claims for the week ended July 18 fell by 22,000 to 187,000, below the estimate of 210,000 and reaching their lowest level since 1969. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, declined by 2,000 to 1.796 million, also trailing the estimate of 1.809 million. Initial claims track new layoffs, while continuing claims measure the number of workers remaining on unemployment benefits.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.82% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Peter Schiff Sounds the Alarm on SpaceX's Junk Debt & SPCX's Post-IPO Stock Crash
data.description:
SpaceX stock (SPCX) closed near $115 on Wednesday, falling almost 20% below its $135 June listing price. At the same time, SpaceX's long-term corporate 2056 bonds yields rose to record highs of 7.6%. The slide in SpaceX's shares and increase in the cost of its debt has brought back worries that bloated valuations across tech stocks and digital assets are starting to unravel.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Economist Peter Schiff highlighted the price fall, calling the slide in Elon Musk's space company a warning for other high-value tech stocks and cryptocurrencies.

SpaceX Debt Trades like High-Risk Junk

In June, SpaceX sold $25 billion in debt across five groups at interest rates between 5.35% and 6.65%. Its longest notes due in 2056 fell the hardest, so SpaceX bonds now trade at prices typical for high-risk junk debt.

Investors are now demanding extra return to hold these long SpaceX notes, with risk premiums climbing from 1.75% at launch to over 2.3%. This jump happened after SpaceX posted a $4.28 billion first-quarter loss on $4.69 billion in revenue. Essentially, it has prompted SpaceX's bondholders to ask for higher yields to cover their risk.

Peter Schiff Points to SpaceX as a Warning

Peter Schiff shared the bond numbers online and pointed out that SpaceX stock now sits roughly 48% below its peak of $225.64. He described the drop as a sign of trouble ahead for overvalued tech companies and cryptocurrencies.

Schiff has long argued that credit stress in the bond market will cause the next big market crash rather than crypto. While Schiff's critics note that his past bearish calls have not always played out, this recent drop in SpaceX's stock price and bond values comes as investors grow more cautious.

SpaceX's Upcoming Stock Unlock Is a Worry for Investors

A major stock unlock date arrives in early August, just two days after SpaceX shares its second-quarter earnings report. Roughly 911.5 million shares, worth about $123 billion at recent prices, will become eligible for sale.

Traders are watching this flood of potential new shares alongside SpaceX's spending plans. We don't know right now whether this weakness will spread into digital assets like Bitcoin. Hopefully, we get more clarity once SpaceX's August earnings and stock unlock pass.

Is SpaceX a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on SPCX stock based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average 12-month SPCX price target of $243.81 per share implies 111.4% upside potential. (See SPCX stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Ramps to 18-Month High as Oil Soars
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield rose 5 basis points to 4.707% on Wednesday, marking its highest level since January 2025.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Rising inflation expectations caused by higher oil prices are behind the uptick. Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, is up by over 6% on the day after the Iran-backed Houthi military group attacked two Saudi tankers attempting to cross the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The Houthis had previously imposed a maritime blockade on Saudi ports, further threatening global energy prices as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted.

Inflation Risks Keep Fed Hike Bets Alive

Higher inflation expectations could push the Fed to keep rates higher for longer. That can drive yields higher as investors adjust to a more hawkish outlook and demand additional compensation for holding government debt.

The Fed's next interest rate decision is scheduled for July 29. The central bank will likely hold rates steady, with the CME FedWatch tool assigning 62.1% odds to the scenario. However, the odds of a 25 bps rate hike have been steadily rising and are at 37.9%, up from 34.2% a day ago and 11.8% a week ago.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Expands Partnership with AI Firm Databricks
data.description:
Technology giant Microsoft (MSFT) has expanded its strategic partnership with artificial intelligence (AI) firm Databricks.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

In a joint news release, the two companies said that they have agreed to extend their collaboration into the 2030s. Databricks will run its core business operations and analytics on Microsoft's Azure cloud computing platform, and will increase its use of Microsoft's Azure Cobalt infrastructure platform.

In return, Microsoft has agreed to deepen its integration of the Databricks data and AI platforms across its products that include Microsoft 365, Teams, Copilot, Power BI, Microsoft Purview, and Microsoft Foundry. Databricks' Genie AI tool and Unity AI Gateway will be integrated into Microsoft customer workflows going forward.

Privately held Databricks is a software company based in San Francisco that is focused on data analytics and AI applications.

Running AI Workloads

"With Databricks Genie and Unity AI Gateway deeply integrated across Microsoft's products, we're helping enterprises unify their data and ground AI in business knowledge," said Ali Ghodsi, CEO of Databricks.

Judson Althoff, CEO of Microsoft's Commercial Business, said Databricks' decision to run its own operations on Azure "gives customers confidence in a platform proven at enterprise scale." Microsoft added that thousands of its customers are already using Databricks on its Azure cloud platform for data analytics and to facilitate AI workloads.

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 37 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 35 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MSFT price target of $558.86 implies 40% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: TSLA, LCID, RIVN: Why Lucid and Rivian Stocks Are Falling Today — July 23, 2026
data.description:
Shares in U.S. electric vehicle makers Lucid (LCID) and Rivian (RIVN) fell on Thursday afternoon. This came as rival Tesla's (TSLA) shares plunged about 14% in afternoon trading following its mixed Q2 2026 earnings released late Wednesday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

TSLQ: built for a short position on TSLA

EV Stocks Fall in Sympathy with Tesla

Lucid's shares fell harder than Rivian's. LCID stock traded more than 7% lower in afternoon trading compared to Rivian's drop of more than 3%.

Tesla's shares came under pressure on Thursday after the EV giant reported a big miss on its earnings per share (EPS). Its EPS came in at $0.33, falling far short of the Wall Street consensus of $0.53.

In addition, Tesla's free cash flow turned negative for the first time in two years. During the quarter, it came in at -$1.1 billion as the automaker's capital spending hit $5.8 billion.

However, the carmaker posted a quarterly record revenue of $28.24 billion. This marked a 26% increase year-over-year.

A Warning to Rivian and Lucid?

During the quarter, Tesla also delivered 480,126 vehicles. This marks a quarterly record and a 25% increase from a year earlier.

However, Tesla had to rely on sharp discounts, low-interest loans, and heavy spending on promotions to expand its deliveries. This drove up Tesla's production costs and dragged down its automotive gross margins. The margins (excluding regulatory credits) fell from 19.2% in the previous quarter to 16.3%.

This indicates a tougher market condition where EV makers might have to sacrifice their profit margins to boost sales. This is important as Lucid and Rivian both remain unprofitable and still need cash to fund their expansion.

Earlier this month, Lucid disclosed that it drew $800 million from the delayed-draw term loan facility provided by its Saudi Arabia backer. Similarly, Rivian recently launched a $1.2 billion stock sale.

Which EV Stock Is the Best Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts currently have a Hold consensus on Tesla (TSLA), Lucid (LCID), and Rivian (RIVN). However, Lucid (LCID) currently leads in terms of upside.

Lucid's average price target of $8.88 implies about 41% upside ahead. This is even though the stock has the lowest Smart Score of the three stocks.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will VZ, AXP, LW Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
On Friday, July 24, Verizon (VZ), American Express (AXP), and Lamb Weston (LW) will release their earnings. All three companies will report before the market open. Traders are already weighing in with their expectations on Polymarket.

Wall Street's Earnings Estimates

  • American Express is expected to report a GAAP EPS of $4.40.
  • Lamb Weston is expected to report an adjusted EPS of 63 cents.

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will Verizon Beat Earnings?

Verizon has beaten or met earnings estimates during 17 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 94.44%. Polymarket gives the wireless communications leader an 86% chance of beating its estimated adjusted EPS of $1.27.

Will American Express Beat Earnings?

American Express has beaten or met earnings estimates during 14 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 77.78%. Polymarket gives the global financial company a 93% chance of beating its estimated GAAP EPS of $4.40.

Will Lamb Weston Beat Earnings?

Lamb Weston has beaten or met earnings estimates during 15 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 83.33%. Polymarket gives the potato processing company an 86% chance of beating its estimated adjusted EPS of 63 cents.

Disclosure: Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: AMD Stock Slips Despite an Expanded OpenAI Partnership
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was down on Thursday even after the semiconductor company updated investors on its partnership with OpenAI during its Advancing AI 2026 event. AMD and OpenAI are expanding their AI partnership beyond hardware supply into full-stack development. The companies are working together across chips, systems, and software to build the infrastructure needed for the next wave of AI models. The effort includes AMD Helios, which OpenAI expects to begin deploying through multiple partners in the second half of 2026.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The partnership started with AMD Instinct MI300X GPUs and grew through MI355X, as both teams worked to improve large-scale systems. Now, AMD Helios combines Instinct MI455X GPUs with 6th Gen AMD EPYC Venice CPUs to support large AI workloads. The Helios rollout will be the first phase of the 6-gigawatt AMD GPU deployment announced in 2025.

"AMD and OpenAI are aligning their roadmaps so future infrastructure is shaped by the workloads it will serve," the companies said. OpenAI provides insight into future model needs, while AMD turns that feedback into new silicon, systems, and software. This approach has helped shape AMD's Instinct MI400 generation and future MI500 products.

AMD and OpenAI are also focused on software tools such as Triton, Gluon, LLVM, and AMD ROCm. For investors, the partnership highlights AMD's push to compete in AI infrastructure by combining its hardware with a growing software ecosystem.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock was down 3.5% today, even with the company's latest AI news. However, investors will note that the shares have still rallied 148.82% year-to-date and have gained 240.69% over the past 12 months.

It also bears mentioning that AMD stock trading activity was light today. As of this writing, more than 15 million shares have changed hands, which is still below the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 28 million shares.

Is AMD Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Strong Buy, based on 28 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $541.31, representing a potential 1.38% upside for the shares. (See AMD Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: RTX Stock Soars After a Guidance Update Boosted Investors' Morale
data.description:
RTX (RTX) stock was up over 7% on Thursday after the aerospace and defense company increased its guidance in its Q2 2026 earnings report. The company raised its adjusted earnings per share guidance to a range of $7.10 to $7.25, compared to its prior outlook range of $6.70 to $6.90. This would see it easily surpass Wall Street's 2026 EPS estimate of $6.92 per share.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

In addition to that EPS guidance boost, RTX also raised its 2026 revenue outlook. The company now expects adjusted sales to come in between $95 billion and $96 billion, compared to its prior guidance of $92.5 billion to $93.5 billion. Yet again, this guidance update has RTX set to beat the analysts' average revenue estimate of $94.11 billion for 2026.

RTX also updated a couple of other guidance metrics in its latest earnings report. That included boosting its organic sales growth guidance to 8% to 9% from 5% to 6%. The company also raised its free cash flow outlook range to $8.50 billion to $8.75 billion from $8.25 billion to $8.75 billion.

RTX Chairman and CEO Chris Calio said, "RTX is exceptionally well positioned to drive continued growth as we execute on our backlog, increase productivity, expand capacity, and introduce new technologies to our customers."

RTX Earnings Beat Estimates

Investors will also note that RTX beat Wall Street's estimates for Q2 2026. The company posted adjusted EPS of $1.89 alongside revenue of $24.71 billion, compared to Wall Street's estimates of $1.66 per share and revenue of $22.89 billion for the quarter.

Calio noted, "RTX delivered very strong second quarter results with 16 percent organic sales growth, including double-digit commercial aftermarket and defense growth, margin expansion across all three segments, and $2.9 billion of free cash flow. Demand remains robust, and our backlog is up 22 percent year over year."

Is RTX Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for RTX is Moderate Buy, based on six Buy and four Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average RTX stock price target of $215.88, representing a potential 3.48% upside for the shares. These ratings and price targets will likely change as analysts update their coverage after today's earnings report. (See RTX Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White
